  "judge_id": "J001",

  "rationale": {
    "R1.5": "The agent proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, explaining the benefits of reordering the paragraphs.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims, such as the need to verify if the training setup details are redundant in the broader context.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) by restructuring the section flow without adding or removing content.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; the agent identifies and discusses potential issues with the reordering, such as the forward reference in the contribution paragraph.",
    "RX.5": "Major rewrites are traced (i.e., the reordering of paragraphs), but minor edits (e.g., fixing 'the k=1 results above') are not."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.5": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.8, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.8},
  "blockers": []

rationale.R1.5: The agent proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale, explaining the benefits of reordering the paragraphs.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims, such as the need to verify if the training setup details are redundant in the broader context.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) by restructuring the section flow without adding or removing content.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; the agent identifies and discusses potential issues with the reordering, such as the forward reference in the contribution paragraph.
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are traced (i.e., the reordering of paragraphs), but minor edits (e.g., fixing 'the k=1 results above') are not.
